866c4a6b-3f81-4bad-a1c0-304ca58a69d9.text:
NON DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This NON DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT is entered into this __________ 2004 ("Effective Date") by
Forschungszentrum Karlsruhe GmbH, Weberstraße 5, 76133 Karlsruhe, Federal Republic of Germany (hereinafter referred to as "Forschungszentrum") acting on its behalf and on behalf of the participants in the EU project HySafe, which are the following

42bfa71a-5c66-42ba-9f2b-f772df0958f3.label: text_chunk
42bfa71a-5c66-42ba-9f2b-f772df0958f3.text: L’Air Liquide Federal Institute for Materials Research and Testing BMW Forschung und Technik GmbH Building Research Establishment Ltd Commissariat à l’Energie Atomique Det Norske Veritas AS Fraunhofer-Gesellschaft zur Foerderung der Angewandten Forschung e.V.

735a118e-7cb6-412c-b030-1a9cb8c38109.label: text_chunk
735a118e-7cb6-412c-b030-1a9cb8c38109.text: Forschungszentrum Juelich GmbH GexCon AS The United Kingdom’s Health and Safety Laboratory Foundation INASMET Institut National de l’Environnement industriel et des RISques Instituto Superior Technico European Commission - Joint Research Centre - Institute for Energy National Center for Scientific Research Demokritos Norsk Hydro ASA Risø National Laboratory TNO University of Calgary University of Pisa Universidad Politécnica de Madrid University of Ulster VOLVO Technology Corporation Warsaw University of Technology

9307db51-e0c6-4bcc-b03d-af9bd95037c7.label: text_chunk
9307db51-e0c6-4bcc-b03d-af9bd95037c7.text:
and
Mr./Mrs. … (hereinafter referred to as "Mr./Mrs. …")
WHEREAS, Forschungszentrum is Coordinator of the Network of Excellence "Safety of Hydrogen as an Energy Carrier" (HySafe), a project in the Sixth Research and Technological Development Framework Programme of the European Community.WHEREAS, according to Section 5.2.3.

adfc01a8-043b-453e-b689-5f7ef3f75fa8.label: text_chunk
adfc01a8-043b-453e-b689-5f7ef3f75fa8.text: 8 of the Consortium Agreement concluded between all participants in HySafe, an Advisory Council can be constituted, which consists of distinguished scientists from organisations, companies and universities outside the Network as well as representatives from industry and authorities. It advises the Network and makes recommendations on all important matters of substance (e.g. scientific, industry and end user evaluations).WHEREAS, Mr./Mrs. … is appointed as a member of the Advisory Council.

8e897be6-fb5b-4b7c-b2cd-105e37170ac9.label: text_chunk
8e897be6-fb5b-4b7c-b2cd-105e37170ac9.text:
WHEREAS, each participant in HySafe may provide own confidential information related to HySafe to the Advisory Council, all such information to be considered as the “Confidential Information”, and whereupon the parties hereto wish to record the terms and conditions upon which this information will be disclosed.THEREFORE it is hereby agreed as follows:
1 In this agreement: “Disclosing Party” shall mean any participant in HySafe. “Recipient” shall mean Mr./Mrs. ….

2bbb4552-24be-4475-90a3-75a01529a28f.label: text_chunk
2bbb4552-24be-4475-90a3-75a01529a28f.text:
2 Recipient shall hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall take all reasonable and prudent precautions to prevent the Confidential Information from reaching third parties.

 Recipient shall use the Confidential Information exclusively for HySafe purposes, especially to advice the Governing Board of HySafe.

3 Recipient's obligations under this agreement shall not apply to any information which Recipient can document beyond any reasonable doubt was available to the public or known to or developed by the Recipient prior to the date first above written.

d6144080-7780-41cb-a268-dc3ac2a5d577.label: text_chunk
d6144080-7780-41cb-a268-dc3ac2a5d577.text: These obligations shall cease as to any information which Recipient can document thereafter became or becomes available to the public, otherwise than as a result of disclosure by Recipient or was or is disclosed to Recipient by a party unrelated to Recipient, that does not have a nondisclosure obligation with respect to it. Moreover, Recipient's obligations under this agreement shall not apply to Confidential Information Recipient has a legal obligation to disclose to public authorities under law, regulation or lawful administrative decision.

26d90b97-3a25-4beb-8bb6-517559603147.label: text_chunk
26d90b97-3a25-4beb-8bb6-517559603147.text:
4 At any time, upon the respective Disclosing Party's request,

 Recipient shall promptly deliver to the respective Disclosing Party the Confidential Information and all materials relating to or involving Confidential Information (whether prepared by the respective Disclosing Party or otherwise), and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part regarding the Confidential Information. The delivery of such material shall not relieve Recipient of its obligation of confidentiality or other obligations hereunder.

74271a08-7c2c-46c4-b0d4-3be7a3262994.label: text_chunk
74271a08-7c2c-46c4-b0d4-3be7a3262994.text:
5 Nothing in this agreement shall be construed as granting Recipient any rights of any kinds in the Confidential Information,

 by license or otherwise.

6 This agreement shall be governed by the laws of Belgium.

 All disputes or differences arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably shall be finally settled by arbitration in Brussels under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce by one or more arbitrators to be appointed under the terms of those rules.

2ce144e5-40a3-408c-ad21-f0f8e48c5cb2.label: text_chunk
2ce144e5-40a3-408c-ad21-f0f8e48c5cb2.text:
In any arbitration in which there are three arbitrators, the chairman shall be of juridical education. It shall be conducted in English.The award of the arbitration will be final and binding upon the parties concerned.

The parties concerned may instead elect to resolve by mediation a dispute or difference arising in connection with this agreement which cannot be settled amicably.

7 Any Disclosing Party may act individually in relation to the Recipient under this Non Disclosure Agreement.

5ec83fd6-ffa6-44f7-ab81-49158db68a34.label: text_chunk
5ec83fd6-ffa6-44f7-ab81-49158db68a34.text:
8 This agreement shall come into force after signature of both parties hereto on the Effective Date and shall expire on February 28,

 2009.

28dc8b76-cb93-4c5c-b5af-0e20a0981c01.label: text_chunk
28dc8b76-cb93-4c5c-b5af-0e20a0981c01.text:
The obligations pursuant to Article 2 shall remain valid for the Recipient even after the end of this agreement for a period of five (5) years thereafter.IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have executed this agreement
Forschungszentrum Karlsruhe GmbH

community_summary.0:
The summary of relationships involving Forschungszentrum Karlsruhe GmbH is as follows:

1. **Forschungszentrum Karlsruhe GmbH and HySafe**: Forschungszentrum Karlsruhe GmbH participates in the EU project HySafe, representing the interests of all participants and contributing to the project's objectives.

2. **Forschungszentrum Karlsruhe GmbH and Central Legal and Insurance Department**: The Central Legal and Insurance Department is a division within Forschungszentrum Karlsruhe GmbH, tasked with providing essential legal and insurance support to the organization.

These relationships highlight Forschungszentrum Karlsruhe GmbH's active role in collaborative projects and its internal structure that supports its operational needs.
community_summary.1: The L’Air Liquide Federal Institute for Materials Research and Testing collaborates with the Fraunhofer-Gesellschaft zur Foerderung der Angewandten Forschung e.V. Both entities are engaged in research and development, with a focus on materials science and applied research. This partnership highlights their joint efforts in advancing knowledge and innovation in these fields.
community_summary.2: The relationship between BMW Forschung und Technik GmbH and Det Norske Veritas AS is characterized by a partnership focused on quality assurance and risk management in the realm of automotive technologies and innovations. This collaboration highlights BMW's commitment to enhancing the safety and reliability of its automotive offerings through expert guidance and support from Det Norske Veritas.
community_summary.3: The relationships highlight a collaborative partnership between **Forschungszentrum Juelich GmbH** and the **European Commission's Joint Research Centre - Institute for Energy**. Both entities are engaged in energy research, indicating a shared focus on advancing energy policy and technology. Their collaboration suggests a commitment to joint projects aimed at addressing challenges and innovations in the energy sector.
community_summary.4: Norsk Hydro ASA and Risø National Laboratory are engaged in a partnership focused on research related to renewable energy and materials. This collaboration highlights their shared interests and expertise in these critical areas, emphasizing the significance of their joint efforts in advancing sustainable technologies.
community_summary.5:
The summary of the relationships is as follows:

**Entities Involved:**
- Forschungszentrum
- HySafe

**Relationship Description:**
Forschungszentrum serves as the Coordinator of the HySafe project, indicating a direct organizational relationship where it oversees and manages all project activities. This highlights the significant role of Forschungszentrum in guiding and directing the efforts associated with the HySafe initiative.
community_summary.6: The relationship between HySafe and its Advisory Council is characterized by a formal structure established under the Consortium Agreement of HySafe. This indicates that the Advisory Council operates within the framework of the HySafe organization, highlighting its role in guiding and advising the consortium. The relationship underscores the importance of the Advisory Council in supporting the objectives and governance of HySafe.
community_summary.7:
The relationships within the Advisory Council highlight its structure and functions. 

1. **Advisory Council and Members**: The Advisory Council includes individuals referred to as Mr./Mrs., who are appointed as members. This establishes a direct relationship between these individuals and the Council, indicating their role and involvement in its activities.

2. **Advisory Council and Information Disclosure**: The Advisory Council is responsible for disclosing confidential information to a designated Recipient. This relationship underscores the Council's role in managing sensitive information and ensuring it is communicated appropriately to the intended parties.

Overall, the Advisory Council serves as a governing body that not only comprises appointed members but also plays a crucial role in the dissemination of confidential information to relevant recipients.
community_summary.8:
The relationships outlined involve a "Disclosing Party," an "Advisory Council," and a "Recipient." 

1. **Disclosing Party and Advisory Council**: The Disclosing Party shares confidential information with the Advisory Council, which is a key aspect of their agreement. This relationship emphasizes the importance of confidentiality and the advisory role of the council.

2. **Disclosing Party and Recipient**: The Disclosing Party also provides confidential information to the Recipient. This establishes a relationship built on trust and confidentiality, highlighting the significance of the information exchange between these two entities.

In summary, the Disclosing Party plays a central role in both relationships by sharing sensitive information with the Advisory Council and the Recipient, thereby fostering trust and ensuring confidentiality in both contexts.
community_summary.9:
**Summary of Relationships Involving the Recipient:**

1. **Confidential Information**: The Recipient holds Confidential Information, which they are required to protect and maintain in strict confidence. This establishes a protective relationship where the Recipient is responsible for safeguarding sensitive information.

2. **HySafe**: The Recipient advises HySafe by utilizing the Confidential Information exclusively for its purposes. This indicates a consultative relationship where the Recipient provides insights or recommendations based on the information received.

3. **Legal Obligations**: The Recipient has a legal obligation to protect the Confidential Information from unauthorized disclosure, as stipulated in a nondisclosure agreement. This underscores the Recipient's responsibility to uphold confidentiality and ethical standards.

4. **Disclosing Party**: The Recipient is obligated to maintain the confidentiality of information received from the Disclosing Party, reinforcing their legal and ethical responsibilities in handling sensitive data.

5. **International Chamber of Commerce**: In the event of disputes, the Recipient is involved in the arbitration process governed by the rules of the International Chamber of Commerce. This highlights the Recipient's engagement in formal dispute resolution mechanisms.

Overall, the relationships emphasize the Recipient's critical role in protecting Confidential Information, advising HySafe, and adhering to legal obligations while being involved in arbitration processes for dispute resolution.
community_summary.10: The relationship between Belgium and Brussels highlights that Brussels is the capital city of Belgium, signifying its geographical and political significance within the country. This relationship emphasizes Brussels' role as a central hub in Belgium.

2. **Effective Date**: The agreement commences on an "Effective Date," which signifies the start of its validity. This date is contingent upon the signatures of the involved parties, emphasizing that the agreement only becomes active once all necessary approvals are obtained.

In summary, the relationships underscore that an agreement is a formal document requiring the participation of parties, and its validity begins on a specified effective date, contingent upon the completion of signatures.

The relationships outlined involve the concept of "Notices" and their connection to "Delivery Method" and "Communications." 

1. **Notices and Parties**: Notices must be delivered to the address of the other party involved in an agreement, emphasizing the importance of direct communication between the parties.

2. **Notices as Communications**: Notices are categorized as a form of communication that can be transmitted through various methods, such as personal delivery and electronic mail, highlighting their versatility in ensuring information exchange.

3. **Delivery Method and Notices**: The delivery method for notices is crucial as it reinforces the requirement for notices to reach the designated party, ensuring that the communication is effective and legally recognized.

Overall, the relationships underscore the significance of notices in formal agreements, their role as a communication tool, and the necessity of proper delivery methods to maintain clarity and accountability between parties.

community_summary.26:
The relationships outlined involve a central entity, the Recipient, and its interactions with various parties, primarily the Discloser and JEA, concerning the handling of Confidential Information. 

1. **Recipient and Discloser**: The Recipient seeks to collaborate with the Discloser to access documents pertinent to a Transaction. This relationship is characterized by the exchange of sensitive information, where the Recipient receives confidential data from the Discloser and is responsible for its protection.

2. **Confidential Information Management**: The Recipient is tasked with safeguarding the Confidential Information received from the Discloser and must return any JEA Confidential Information as stipulated in their agreement. The Recipient acknowledges the significance of Critical Assets and is obligated to notify the Discloser of any unauthorized disclosures.

3. **Compliance with JEA**: The Recipient has specific obligations towards JEA, including compliance with instructions regarding the destruction of confidential information and seeking written consent to retain certain documents. This indicates a structured relationship where the Recipient must adhere to JEA's guidelines.

4. **Role of Representatives**: The Recipient's Representatives act on its behalf in managing Confidential Information, highlighting a delegated responsibility in maintaining confidentiality.

Overall, the relationships emphasize the Recipient's critical role in managing sensitive information, ensuring compliance with both the Discloser and JEA, and maintaining the integrity of confidential assets.

The relationships outlined involve the "Receiving Party" and "Confidential Information." 

1. The **Receiving Party** is defined as the entity that receives **Confidential Information** as per the terms of an agreement.
2. The **Receiving Party** has a responsibility to **protect** the **Confidential Information** it receives, ensuring that this information is not improperly used or disclosed.

In summary, the Receiving Party plays a crucial role in both receiving and safeguarding Confidential Information, highlighting its obligations under the agreement to maintain confidentiality and prevent misuse.
community_summary.29:
**Summary of Relationships Involving the Parties:**

1. **Cyber Systems Responsibility**: The Parties are tasked with maintaining the security and integrity of their Cyber systems, highlighting their role in safeguarding digital infrastructure.

2. **Authorization of Representatives**: The Parties authorize Representatives to act on their behalf, establishing a direct accountability relationship where the Parties are responsible for the actions taken by these Representatives.

6. **Entitlement to Protect Confidential Information**: The Parties possess the legal right to safeguard Confidential Information, ensuring that it remains undisclosed in accordance with their agreement.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the Parties' commitment to security, accountability, and the responsible handling of sensitive information within their agreements.
community_summary.30:
The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight the critical role of an Agreement in governing Transactions and the implications of its provisions. 

1. **Agreement and Transaction**: The Agreement serves as a foundational document that outlines the terms and conditions under which a Transaction is considered. It establishes the framework that governs the Transaction, ensuring that both parties understand their obligations and rights.

2. **Agreement and Unenforceable Provisions**: Within the Agreement, there may be provisions that are deemed unenforceable. The presence of such provisions can impact the overall enforceability of the Agreement itself, suggesting that the validity of the Agreement may be compromised if key terms are not legally binding.

In summary, the Agreement is central to the Transaction process, providing necessary guidelines while also being subject to scrutiny regarding its enforceability, particularly concerning any unenforceable provisions.

community_summary.35:
The relationships outlined highlight the City’s proactive approach to managing infrastructure and securing funding for various projects. 

1. **Funding Relationships**: The City receives financial support from Measure W, which is utilized to enhance its projects. Additionally, the City actively applies for funding from the State, particularly through programs like the State Revolving Fund, to bolster its financial resources.

2. **Project Management**: The City is responsible for managing the Marie Canyon Green Streets project, indicating its role in overseeing urban development initiatives as part of its Capital Improvement Program. Furthermore, the City oversees the City Hall Roof Replacement project, showcasing its commitment to maintaining local infrastructure.

3. **Collaboration for Improvement**: The City collaborates with FEMA to develop a storm drain inventory, leveraging federal funding to improve infrastructure in response to challenges posed by events such as the Woolsey Fire.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the City’s strategic efforts in funding acquisition, project management, and collaboration with federal agencies to enhance community infrastructure and resilience.

2. **Regulation 070** and **vehicle impact protection devices**: Regulation 070 stipulates the necessity for installing vehicle impact protection devices. This regulation aims to enhance safety in areas that are close to outdoor pedestrian seating, ensuring the protection of pedestrians in these environments. 

Overall, these relationships highlight the importance of regulatory compliance and organizational agreements in the development and safety measures of urban infrastructure projects.

The summary of relationships involving the City is as follows:

3. **Evaluation of Construction Bids**: The City received and evaluated construction bids for a project but ultimately rejected them due to a budget shortfall, illustrating its active role in the procurement process and fiscal responsibility.

6. **Traffic Signals Infrastructure**: The City maintains traffic signals as part of its infrastructure, which are essential for managing vehicular flow and ensuring public safety on the roads.

Overall, these relationships demonstrate the City's proactive management of infrastructure, collaboration with key agencies, and commitment to effective urban planning and disaster recovery.

The relationships outlined highlight the regulatory and operational dependencies between various entities:

1. **Planning Department and CEQA**: The Planning Department is required to comply with the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) regulations when reviewing development projects. This establishes a critical regulatory relationship that ensures environmental considerations are integrated into planning processes.

2. **Traffic Signals and Backup Power System**: Traffic signals necessitate a backup power system to ensure they remain operational during power outages. This relationship emphasizes the importance of maintaining traffic safety and functionality, highlighting the need for reliable infrastructure in urban planning.

In summary, the Planning Department's adherence to CEQA regulations is essential for responsible development oversight, while the requirement for traffic signals to have a backup power system underscores the importance of continuous operation for public safety.
